channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
item.description: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.
item.content:encoded:
The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: North Korea says its latest weapons tests included missiles with cluster-bomb warheads
item.description: North Korea said its testing spree this week involved various new weapons systems, including ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads, as it pushes to expand nuclear-capable forces.
item.content:encoded:
North Korea said its testing spree this week involved various new weapons systems, including ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads, as it pushes to expand nuclear-capable forces.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
item.description: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.
item.content:encoded:
A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Teen birth rates hit another historical low in 2025, CDC says
item.description: The teen birth rate continues its decades-long downward trend. Researchers say many factors are at play, including less sexual activity and more access to contraception and abortion.
item.content:encoded:
The teen birth rate continues its decades-long downward trend. Researchers say many factors are at play, including less sexual activity and more access to contraception and abortion.

(Image credit: Kena Betancur)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Bill Gates will testify in the Epstein probe; Pam Bondi testimony postponed
item.description: Bill Gates will appear before the House Oversight Committee in June. The Department of Justice said Wednesday that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not testify for now.
item.content:encoded:
Bill Gates will appear before the House Oversight Committee in June. The Department of Justice said Wednesday that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not testify for now.

(Image credit: Roy Rochlin)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Birthplace of civil rights and confederacy cradle: Montgomery is packed with history
item.description: Montgomery, Ala., helped shape America — from the cradle of the Confederacy to the birthplace of the civil rights movement to the city's draw today as the place to reflect on the legacy of slavery.
item.content:encoded: Montgomery, Ala., helped shape America — from the cradle of the Confederacy to the birthplace of the civil rights movement to the city's draw today as the place to reflect on the legacy of slavery.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: What has the U.S. war with Iran accomplished?
item.description: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Why OpenAI bought 'SportsCenter for Silicon Valley'
item.description: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.content:encoded: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
item.description: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from the past.
item.content:encoded: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from the past.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: 11 new books in April offer a chance to step inside someone else's world
item.description: The books we're spotlighting this month don't exactly radiate escapist good vibes — but they do offer the opportunity to step into someone else's life and get to know their view of our shared world.
item.content:encoded: The books we're spotlighting this month don't exactly radiate escapist good vibes — but they do offer the opportunity to step into someone else's life and get to know their view of our shared world.
item.dc:creator: Colin Dwyer
